workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Just came out of account health with Fabrikam Residences. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: account health Discussion - Fabrikam Residences
Date: 2024-01-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is account health.

[00:08:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:11:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:15:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:23:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:28:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is cost optimization.

[00:31:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:33:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:35:00] Kevin Lee (Fabrikam Residences): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-G trap (Invents action items from speculative discussion); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-G failure mode (Invents action items from speculative discussion)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-G -- Invents action items from speculative discussion
